_protocol: Phase 3.5-PRAGMATIC for UN_BACKTESTABLE topic: 5+red+5 fetches with reference-class analogs + adversarial gap review
_critical_state_change_since_topic_desig: Topic was designed assuming Hormuz would still be open or partially open with reopening as the question. As of Round 1 fetch May 2026, Hormuz CLOSED since Feb 28 2026 after US+Israel strikes against Iran killed Khamenei. Transit ~10% of normal. US blockade of Iranian ports. Ground truth has shifted dramatically — topic priors require major revision.

extracted.trigger: US+Israel strikes against Iran; Khamenei assassinated
extracted.current_transit_volume: ~10% of normal (12 crossings May 2 vs ~120/day pre-crisis)
extracted.iran_april_8_ceasefire_status: conditional ceasefire declared but unclear hold

extracted.suez_1967_1975: 8 YEARS — Egypt closed in war with Israel; reopened only after political settlement
extracted._pattern: Wide bimodal distribution — quick reopens (months) when political settlement quick, very long closures (years) when entrenched conflict

extracted.april_2025_initial_negotiations_60_day_d: Trump letter to Khamenei; missed deadline → Israel strikes
extracted.april_12_us_blockade_announced: Pentagon announces blockade of Iranian ports

extracted._implication: Severe economic pressure on Iran for resolution; regime survival pressure
Premium has eased 50% from peaks but remains 5-15x pre-crisis

gaps_identified: Chokepoint reference class N=3 too narrow — missing Shatt al-Arab 1980-88 (closely analogous)
Post-Khamenei IRGC decision-making structure not characterized — Powell-analog empty
Iran 14-point proposal contents not extracted — H2 trajectory unscored
Israeli operational posture / war aims not covered
China leverage / strategic appetite for fast vs slow reopen unquantified

extracted.trigger: Saddam invaded Iran Sep 22 1980 abrogating Algiers Protocol
extracted.reopening: Pre-1980 status quo restored after war ended 1988
extracted.navigability: Cluttered with sunken ship hulks for entire war
extracted._analog_relevance_to_hormuz: Strong structural analog: Iran party, regime survival, Gulf chokepoint, Western intervention. CLOSURE LASTED ENTIRE WAR DURATION — heavy H4 evidence if Iran-US/Israel conflict drags. BUT: Hormuz is not a battleground waterway like Shatt al-Arab was, China demands transit, and Iran economy more pressured now

extracted.new_supreme_leader: Mojtaba Khamenei (chosen by IRGC pressure on Assembly of Experts)
extracted.actual_decision_authority: Collective IRGC military council; Mojtaba 'coordinating figure not sole authority'
extracted.mojtaba_described_as: more pliant than father; backs IRGC hardline
extracted._implication_for_de_escalation: Decision authority diffuse — no single 'Khomeini drinks poison' figure. Could shorten reopen timeline (collective pragmatism on economic pressure) OR lengthen it (no single decision-maker can override hardliners)

core_demands: 30-day deadline to resolve issues (not 2-month US ceasefire)
US troop withdrawal from Iran's periphery
New mechanism governing Strait of Hormuz
extracted.nuclear_issues: EXCLUDED from this proposal — deferred to post-war negotiations
extracted.trump_response: 'not acceptable'; threatened resumed strikes if Iran 'misbehaves'
extracted._implication_for_h2: Realistic dealing-track exists but core distance between sides remains large; H2 (by Q1 2027) requires substantial give from BOTH sides in next ~10 months

extracted.stated_war_aim: Regime change / 'state collapse' (per Daniel Levy quote)
extracted.netanyahu_position: Continues strikes in Iran AND Lebanon; coalition depends on hard-right
extracted.april_8_ceasefire_status: Conditional; Netanyahu reserves right to strike
extracted._implication: Israeli veto/spoiler risk for any reopen deal. Israel less constrained by US than typical alliance dynamic. Major H4 driver if Israeli strikes continue.

extracted.china_dependency_on_iranian_oil: ~half of Chinese seaborne oil imports through Hormuz; 90% of Iranian oil to China
extracted.iran_selective_transit_announcement_mar_: China, Russia, India, Iraq, Pakistan ships allowed to transit
extracted._china_appetite: Strong incentive for fast reopen — energy security + economic

reference_class.definition: Military-induced maritime chokepoint closures with at least partial closure and external power involvement

analog_events.name: Suez 1967-1975
analog_events.context: Egypt-Israel war + occupied Sinai; required political settlement

analog_events.name: Shatt al-Arab 1980-1988
analog_events.context: Iran-Iraq war; closed entire war duration

analog_events.name: Bab-el-Mandeb 2023-2025
analog_events.context: Houthi disruption; resolved by ceasefire late 2025

exclusions.reason: Brief (days); not appropriate analog for 30-day-baseline reopen criterion
High-intensity disruption but not full closure

topic_priors.rationale: 10-source corpus shows: (a) Active war ongoing despite April 8 conditional ceasefire — H1 (full reopen by Sep 2026) constrained by Israeli regime-change posture and US blockade. (b) Iran economic pressure (rial 1.81M, IMF -6.1% GDP, 68.9% inflation, food 105%) creates strong incentive for resolution within 6-12 months supporting H2/H3. (c) China+Pakistan mediation active; China economic pressure for reopen. (d) Iran 14-point plan offers realistic deal trajectory but Trump rejected; substantial give needed from both sides. (e) Reference class bimodal: Suez 1956 (5mo), Bab-el-Mandeb (24mo), vs Suez 1967 (8yr), Shatt al-Arab (8yr). Modal-prior closures resolved by political settlement. Net: H1 down 3pp (Israel/US blockade headwind), H3 up 5pp (more time helps if H2 deal slips), H4 down 2pp (Iran economic pressure makes 8-year-Shatt-analog less likely than otherwise).
